notes: Signal and sensor blocks: antennas broadcast communications; ore detectors scan for buried ores. Both antenna variants advertise a 10 km broadcast range. Ore detectors have a per-ore filter and default to broadcasting found markers via any attached antenna. Build-component quantities are totals (initial + extra welding phases). Note: the Radio-Comm Module's usedIn list on the wiki claims Antenna 12.5 m and Ore Detector 5 m consume it, but the individual block pages do not list Radio-Comm Module in their build components — this repo follows the per-block pages as authoritative and flags the inconsistency. Every detection block reports power.maxInput=0; almost certainly a wiki omission (antennas/detectors need power).

resources.description: Provides long-range communication between grids and allows remote access to them.
resources.unlock: Unlocked by default in all survival worlds

resources.description: Provides long-range communication between grids and allows remote access to them.
dataGaps: buildComponents (Radio-Comm Module's usedIn lists this block, but this block's page does not list radio_comm_module as a build component — wiki inconsistency)

resources.description: Detects natural resources (raw ores) on asteroids and planets. Typically paired with drills for mining operations; markers can be broadcast via an attached antenna.
resources.unlock: Unlocked by default in all survival worlds

resources.description: Detects natural resources (raw ores) on asteroids and planets. Markers can be broadcast via an attached antenna.
dataGaps: buildComponents (Radio-Comm Module and Permanent Magnet both appear in conflicting wiki sources; this entry follows the Ore Detector 5 m block page which lists only Steel Plate and Permanent Magnet — flagged because the Radio-Comm Module page claims this block as a consumer)
